structured.name: Authorization using PIN-code
structured.objective: To make it possible for a Charging Station that has a key entry terminal to authorize the PIN-code.
structured.description:
When a Charging Station has a PIN-code entry terminal, an EV driver enters his/her PIN-code. This
PIN-code is sent to the CSMS for validation using an AuthorizeRequest.
scenario:
1. The EV Driver wants to start or stop charging the EV and enters his/her PIN-code into the
terminal.

2. The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest message, with the field idToken.type set to
KeyCode, to the CSMS to request authorization.

3. Upon receipt of the AuthorizeRequest, the CSMS responds with an AuthorizeResponse. This
response indicates whether or not the KeyCode is accepted by the CSMS.
structured.prerequisites: Charging Station has a PIN-code entry terminal to start charging of an EV.
structured.postconditions: Transaction ongoing on Charging Station, CSMS is aware of transaction.
structured.remarks:
When the PIN-code is validated in the Charging Station, instead of the CSMS, use case C02 -
Authorization Using a Start button applies.

requirements.precondition:
When the CSMS receives an AuthorizeRequest
with a keyCode that is not valid at this Charging
Station
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse message
with status = Invalid.

requirements.precondition:
When the CSMS receives an AuthorizeRequest
with a keyCode that is valid and the EV Driver is
allowed to charge at this Charging Station
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse message
with status = Accepted.

A Charging Station MAY store keyCodes in the Authorization
Cache.

requirements.precondition: If an idToken of type keyCode is used
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station or CSMS SHALL NOT show the IdToken in
any logging. key codes should never appear in logs.

Language SHALL be specified as RFC-5646 tags, see: [RFC5646],
for example: US English is: "en-US".

requirements.precondition: If an idToken of type keyCode is used
requirements.definition:
It is RECOMMENDED to take measures to prevent brute force
attacks, for example by increasing backoff times after attempts
to enter an incorrect keyCode.

chunks.content:
# C04 - Authorization using PIN-code

**Objective:** To make it possible for a Charging Station that has a key entry terminal to authorize the PIN-code.

**Description:** When a Charging Station has a PIN-code entry terminal, an EV driver enters his/her PIN-code. This
PIN-code is sent to the CSMS for validation using an AuthorizeRequest.

**Actors:** EV Driver, Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The EV Driver wants to start or stop charging the EV and enters his/her PIN-code into the
terminal.
2. The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest message, with the field idToken.type set to
KeyCode, to the CSMS to request authorization.
3. Upon receipt of the AuthorizeRequest, the CSMS responds with an AuthorizeResponse. This
response indicates whether or not the KeyCode is accepted by the CSMS.

**Prerequisites:** Charging Station has a PIN-code entry terminal to start charging of an EV.

**Postconditions:** Transaction ongoing on Charging Station, CSMS is aware of transaction.

**Remarks:** When the PIN-code is validated in the Charging Station, instead of the CSMS, use case C02 -
Authorization Using a Start button applies.

# C04 - Requirements

**C04.FR.01**
Precondition: When the CSMS receives an AuthorizeRequest
with a keyCode that is not valid at this Charging
Station
The CSMS SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse message
with status = Invalid.

**C04.FR.02**
Precondition: When the CSMS receives an AuthorizeRequest
with a keyCode that is valid and the EV Driver is
allowed to charge at this Charging Station
The CSMS SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse message
with status = Accepted.

**C04.FR.03**
A Charging Station MAY store keyCodes in the Authorization
Cache.

**C04.FR.04**
Precondition: If an idToken of type keyCode is used
The Charging Station or CSMS SHALL NOT show the IdToken in
any logging. key codes should never appear in logs.

**C04.FR.05**
Language SHALL be specified as RFC-5646 tags, see: [RFC5646],
for example: US English is: "en-US".

**C04.FR.06**
Precondition: If an idToken of type keyCode is used
It is RECOMMENDED to take measures to prevent brute force
attacks, for example by increasing backoff times after attempts
to enter an incorrect keyCode.
